IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: ROTARIANS WORK TOWARD ADVANCING WORLD UNDERSTANDING, GOODWILL AND PEACE.
IRS990.MissionDesc: THE MISSION OF THE ROTARY FOUNDATION OF ROTARY INTERNATIONAL IS TO ENABLE ROTARIANS TO ADVANCE WORLD UNDERSTANDING, GOODWILL, AND PEACE THROUGH THE IMPROVEMENT OF HEALTH, THE SUPPORT OF EDUCATION, AND THE ALLEVIATION OF POVERTY.
IRS990.Desc: POLIOPLUS - THE POLIOPLUS PROGRAM IS ROTARY'S EFFORT TO ERADICATE THE POLIOVIRUS WORLDWIDE. ROTARIANS HAVE PROVIDED FINANCIAL SUPPORT AND COUNTLESS VOLUNTEER HOURS TO ENSURE THAT CHILDREN ARE IMMUNIZED AGAINST THIS CRIPPLING DISEASE AND THAT SURVEILLANCE OF THE VIRUS IS STRONG, DESPITE THE POOR INFRASTRUCTURE, EXTREME POVERTY, AND CIVIL STRIFE OF MANY COUNTRIES. FUNDS SUPPORT NATIONAL IMMUNIZATION DAYS, POLIOVIRUS TRANSMISSION MONITORING, AND OTHER EFFORTS IN POLIO-ENDEMIC, RECENTLY ENDEMIC, AND HIGH RISK COUNTRIES. ROTARY IS A CORE PARTNER IN THE GLOBAL POLIO ERADICATION INITIATIVE, ALONG WITH THE WORLD HEALTH ORGANIZATION, UNICEF, THE CDC, AND THE BILL AND MELINDA GATES FOUNDATION.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: ROTARY GRANTS - GLOBAL GRANTS SUPPORT HUMANITARIAN PROJECTS, SCHOLARSHIPS, AND VOCATIONAL TRAINING TEAMS IN ROTARY'S AREAS OF FOCUS.DISTRICT GRANTS SUPPORT SMALLER-SCALE PROJECTS RELATED TO THE FOUNDATION'S MISSION. PARTNERSHIP GRANTS ALSO SUPPORT THE AREAS OF FOCUS AND ARE CARRIED OUT WITH THE FOUNDATION'S STRATEGIC PARTNERS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: DONOR ADVISED FUND - THE DONOR ADVISED FUND IS A CHARITABLE GIVING VEHICLE THAT OFFERS INDIVIDUALS AND ROTARY-AFFILIATED GROUPS THE ABILITY TO MAKE GRANT RECOMMENDATIONS TO SUPPORT EITHER THE ROTARY FOUNDATION PROGRAMS OR OTHER IRS-APPROVED CHARITIES. THE ROTARY FOUNDATION HAS ULTIMATE DISCRETION AND CONTROL OVER DISTRIBUTION OF THE FUNDS.

ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp.Desc: DISASTER RESPONSE GRANTS - THE ROTARY FOUNDATION SUPPORTS DISASTER RELIEF AND RECOVERY EFFORTS THROUGH GRANTS FROM THE ROTARY DISASTER RESPONSE FUND.ROTARY PEACE CENTERS - EACH YEAR, UP TO 100 FELLOWS ARE SPONSORED TO STUDY AT ONE OF THE ROTARY PEACE CENTERS FOR A MASTER'S-LEVEL DEGREE OR PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT CERTIFICATE.SPECIAL INITIATIVE GRANTS - OTHER PROGRAM SERVICES THAT ARE IN PLACE TO COORDINATE AND DIRECT THE ACTIVITIES OF THE ROTARY FOUNDATION.PROJECT ENHANCEMENT PROCESS - PROVIDES PROJECT PLANNING AND TECHNICAL SUPPORT TO CLUBS AND DISTRICTS FOR GLOBAL GRANTS

ForeignAddress.AddressLine1Txt: C/O STATE STREET PO BOX 896 1 NEX

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE A, PART II, LINE 10, EXPLANATION OF OTHER INCOME:

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ROTARY FOUNDATION'S ENDOWMENT FUND ENSURES A STRONG FUTURE FOR THE FOUNDATION BY PROVIDING A CONTINUOUS STREAM OF INCOME TO MEET THE INCREASING DEMAND FOR FOUNDATION PROGRAMS IN PERPETUITY.

FIN 48 (ASC 740) FOOTNOTE THE FOUNDATION HAS RECEIVED A FAVORABLE DETERMINATION LETTER FROM THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE ("IRS") STATING THAT THE FOUNDATION IS EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL INCOME TAXES UNDER THE PROVISIONS OF SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE OF 1986, AS AMENDED, EXCEPT FOR INCOME TAXES PERTAINING TO UNRELATED BUSINESS INCOME. TAX EFFECTS FROM UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS ARE RECOGNIZED IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ONLY IF THE POSITION IS MORE LIKELY THAN NOT TO BE SUSTAINED IF THE POSITION WERE TO BE CHALLENGED BY A TAXING AUTHORITY. MANAGEMENT HAS DETERMINED THERE ARE NO MATERIAL UNCERTAIN POSITIONS THAT REQUIRE RECOGNITION IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. ADDITIONALLY, NO PROVISION FOR INCOME TAXES IS REFLECTED IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND THERE IS NO INTEREST OR PENALTIES RECOGNIZED IN THE STATEMENTS OF ACTIVITIES OR STATEMENTS OF FINANCIAL POSITION.
LOSS ON CASH SURRENDER VALUE OF INSURANCE OF $19,555 19,555.
CHANGE IN ACTUARIAL VALUE OF SPLIT-INTEREST AGREEMENTS OF $2,094,128 3,626,327. OFFICE OF INVESTMENT EXPENSES OF $1,514,458 INCOME TAXES OF $17,722 VARIOUS BOOK/TAX DIFFERENCE OF $19
REVERSAL OF GRANT EXPENSES -6,460,402. ADJUSTMENT OF NET ASSETS 1,482.
RESERVE FOR PLEDGES OF $2,375,407 3,907,587. CIO EXPENSES OF $1,514,458 INCOME TAXES OF $17,722

PROCEDURE FOR MONITORING USE OF GRANT FUNDS OUTSIDE THE U.S. THE ROTARY FOUNDATION MONITORS AND AUDITS GRANTS BY VISITING PROJECT SITES AND REVIEWING PROGRESS REPORTS THROUGHOUT THE FISCAL YEAR. SUCH ACTION HELPS THE ROTARY FOUNDATION DETERMINE THE TECHNICAL FEASIBILITY OF PROPOSED GRANT PROJECTS, PERFORM FINANCIAL EVALUATIONS, MONITOR THE PROGRESS OF THE PROJECTS, EVALUATE PROJECT MANAGEMENT, MEASURE BENEFITS AND RESULTS, AND ENSURE APPROPRIATE USE OF ALL ROTARY FOUNDATION FUNDS. RANDOM AUDITS ARE ALSO EXECUTED EACH YEAR.
THE ACCRUAL METHOD IS USED TO REPORT EXPENDITURES FOR GRANT MAKING AND UNRELATED TRADE OR BUSINESS. GRANT DISTRIBUTIONS ARE INCLUDED IN THE PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENDITURES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE F, PART I, LINE 3, COLUMN (E)
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DESCRIPTION 1: BASIC EDUCATION AND LITERACY, DISASTER RESPONSE, DISEASE PREVENTION AND TREATMENT, ECONOMIC AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH, PEACE AND CONFLICT PREVENTION/RESOLUTION, WATER AND SANITATION. DESCRIPTION 2: DISASTER RESPONSE, DISEASE PREVENTION AND TREATMENT, ECONOMIC AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH, PEACE AND CONFLICT PREVENTION/RESOLUTION, WATER AND SANITATION. DESCRIPTION 3: DISASTER RESPONSE, DISEASE PREVENTION AND TREATMENT, ECONOMIC AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH, PEACE AND CONFLICT PREVENTION/RESOLUTION.
THE ROTARY FOUNDATION SUPPLIES OR REIMBURSES FOR STANDARD FUNDRAISING EXPENSES. THE AMOUNT PAID TO REIMBURSE EXPENSES SUCH AS TRAVEL, CELL PHONE USAGE AND POSTAGE WAS $47,511.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PROCEDURE FOR MONITORING USE OF GRANT FUNDS INSIDE U.S. THE ROTARY FOUNDATION MONITORS AND AUDITS GRANTS BY VISITING PROJECT SITES AND REVIEWING PROGRESS REPORTS THROUGHOUT THE FISCAL YEAR. SUCH ACTION HELPS THE ROTARY FOUNDATION DETERMINE THE TECHNICAL FEASIBILITY OF PROPOSED GRANT PROJECTS, PERFORM FINANCIAL EVALUATIONS, MONITOR THE PROGRESS OF THE PROJECTS, EVALUATE PROJECT MANAGEMENT, MEASURE BENEFITS AND RESULTS, AND ENSURE APPROPRIATE USE OF ALL ROTARY FOUNDATION FUNDS. RANDOM AUDITS ARE ALSO EXECUTED EACH YEAR.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, SCHEDULE J, PART I, LINE 1A - FIRST CLASS TRAVEL
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: UNCOMPENSATED VOLUNTEER LEADERS CONDUCT EXTENSIVE INTERNATIONAL TRAVEL ON BEHALF OF THE ORGANIZATION. THE ROTARY FOUNDATION PROVIDES BUSINESS-CLASS AIRFARE FOR THE TRUSTEES AND THE GENERAL SECRETARY. BUSINESS-CLASS IS AVAILABLE IN MOST MARKETS, BUT IF IT IS NOT, FIRST-CLASS OR ECONOMY AIRFARES ARE SUBSTITUTED. THE ROTARY FOUNDATION TRUSTEE CHAIR IS PERMITTED TO USE FIRST-CLASS TRAVEL, ALTHOUGH HE MAY CHOOSE BUSINESS-CLASS OR ECONOMY CLASS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, SCHEDULE J, PART 1, LINE 1A - TRAVEL FOR COMPANIONS
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ROTARY FOUNDATION PROVIDES FOR SPOUSE TRAVEL IF SPOUSE PARTICIPATION ASSISTS THE ORGANIZATION IN ACHIEVING ITS MISSION. THE ROTARIAN AND SPOUSE ARE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT DOCUMENTATION DETAILING THE ACTIVITIES AND SUPPORTING THE BONA FIDE BUSINESS PURPOSE OF THE TRAVEL. MANAGEMENT REVIEWS THE DOCUMENTATION DURING THE EXPENSE REIMBURSEMENT APPROVAL PROCESS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, SCHEDULE J, PART I LINE 1A - HIGHEST COMPENSATED EMPLOYEES
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ROTARY FOUNDATION HAS A HIGHEST COMPENSATED EMPLOYEE AT ITS OFFICE IN ZURICH, SWITZERLAND, WHICH IS CURRENTLY UNDISCLOSED DUE TO POTENTIAL INFRINGEMENT OF LOCAL DATA PRIVACY LAWS. SALARIES FOR STAFF AT THE INTERNATIONAL OFFICES ARE ESTABLISHED IN THEIR LOCAL CURRENCY AND TRANSLATED TO US DOLLARS FOR REPORTING PURPOSES, RESULTING IN POTENTIALLY LARGE FLUCTUATIONS IN THE US DOLLAR REPORTABLE EQUIVALENT FOR COMPENSATION.
THE FOUNDATION IS REPORTING THE NUMBER OF ITEMS CONTRIBUTED BY TYPE, PER CONTRIBUTOR.
INVESTMENT FIRMS ACCEPTED AND LIQUIDATED PUBLICLY TRADED SECURITIES ON THE FOUNDATION'S BEHALF.

NUMBER OF EMPLOYEES ON W-3 THE NUMBER OF EMPLOYEES REPORTED IS THE TOTAL EMPLOYEE COUNT FROM THE FILING ORGANIZATION. WHILE ROTARY INTERNATIONAL IS THE COMMON PAYMASTER FOR ROTARY INTERNATIONAL AND THE ROTARY FOUNDATION OF ROTARY INTERNATIONAL (TRF), THE NUMBER OF EMPLOYEES DOES NOT INCLUDE THE EMPLOYEE COUNT FOR ROTARY INTERNATIONAL.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ROTARY INTERNATIONAL (RI) IS THE SOLE MEMBER OF THE ROTARY FOUNDATION (TRF).

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ROTARY INTERNATIONAL (RI) IS THE SOLE MEMBER OF THE ROTARY FOUNDATION (TRF). ANNUALLY, THE RI PRESIDENT, WITH THE CONCURRENCE OF THE RI BOARD OF DIRECTORS, APPOINTS TRUSTEES OF TRF TO SUCCEED THOSE WHOSE TERMS ARE EXPIRING AND TO FILL ANY VACANCIES THAT HAVE OCCURRED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ROTARY FOUNDATION'S FORM 990 IS PREPARED BY AN INDEPENDENT CERTIFIED PUBLIC ACCOUNTING FIRM. A DRAFT OF THE FORM 990 IS REVIEWED BY THE DIRECTOR OF FINANCE AND THE CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER. UPON COMPLETING OF THE REVIEW PROCESS, THE RETURN IS PROVIDED TO THE TRUSTEES, SIGNED BY THE CFO AND FILED WITH THE IRS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL TRUSTEES MUST DISCLOSE ANY FAMILY OR BUSINESS RELATIONSHIPS WITH OTHER TRUSTEES, ROTARY INTERNATIONAL DIRECTORS, KEY EMPLOYEES, OR HIGHEST COMPENSATED INDEPENDENT CONTRACTORS OF THE ROTARY FOUNDATION AS IDENTIFIED ANNUALLY BY THE GENERAL SECRETARY. TO COMPLY WITH THIS POLICY, TRUSTEES SUBMIT AN ANNUAL POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST STATEMENT, ON WHICH THEY REPORT ANY PREVIOUSLY UNDISCLOSED POTENTIAL CONFLICTS OF INTEREST. THE ROTARY FOUNDATION EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE REVIEWS THESE REPORTS AND WORKS TO RESOLVE ANY ACTUAL OR POTENTIAL CONFLICTS. IF NO RESOLUTION IS REACHED, THE COMMITTEE REFERS THE MATTER TO THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES AND AN APPROPRIATE ACTION WILL BE TAKEN. A POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST IS DEEMED TO EXIST IF A MAJORITY OF TRUSTEES VOTING REACH AN AFFIRMATIVE DECISION. THE TRUSTEE WITH THE POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST SHALL NOT BE PRESENT FOR THE VOTE. KEY EMPLOYEES AND OTHER EMPLOYEES IN A POSITION OF INFLUENCE ARE ALSO REQUIRED TO MAKE ANNUAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCLOSURES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL US EMPLOYEES ARE EMPLOYED BY ROTARY INTERNATIONAL (RI), A RELATED ORGANIZATION OF THE ROTARY FOUNDATION. THE GLOBAL PEOPLE & TALENT TEAM COLLECTS DATA ANNUALLY ON TOTAL COMPENSATION (I.E., BASE SALARY AND BENEFITS) FROM SEVERAL SOURCES, INCLUDING INDEPENDENT COMPENSATION CONSULTANTS, SALARY SURVEYS, PROFESSIONAL PUBLICATIONS, AND INFORMATION FROM SIMILAR ORGANIZATIONS IN THE SAME GEOGRAPHICAL AREA. THIS PROCESS IS CONTEMPORANEOUSLY DOCUMENTED AND WAS LAST UNDERTAKEN IN 2020. PEOPLE & TALENT PREPARES A REPORT FOR THE OPERATIONS REVIEW COMMITTEE ON THE SALARY RANGES FOR THE POSITIONS OF GENERAL SECRETARY (CEO), DEPUTY GENERAL SECRETARY, GENERAL MANAGERS, AND CHIEF INVESTMENTS OFFICER. THE OPERATIONS REVIEW COMMITTEE OF RI (ACTING AS A COMPENSATION ADVISORY COMMITTEE) RECEIVES AND REVIEWS THE REPORT ON COMPENSATION COMPARABILITY DATA, REVIEWS PAY RANGES RECOMMENDED, CONFIRMS THE REASONABLENESS OF COMPENSATION PROPOSED TO BE PAID IN THE COMING YEAR TO ROTARY'S DISQUALIFIED PERSONS, AND DOCUMENTS ANY EXCEPTIONS. THE OPERATIONS REVIEW COMMITTEE PROVIDE INFORMATION TO THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR REVIEW AND APPROVAL OF REVIEW SALARY RANGES AND SALARY INCREASE PARAMETERS FOR REASONABLENESS. THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE OF THE TRUSTEES OF THE ROTARY FOUNDATION (TRF) ALSO REVIEW FOR REASONABLENESS THE AMOUNTS PAID FOR THE SERVICES OF THE GENERAL SECRETARY (CEO), DEPUTY GENERAL SECRETARY, GENERAL MANAGERS, AND CHIEF INVESTMENTS OFFICER DURING THE NEXT YEAR, TO ASSURE THAT SUCH PAYMENTS ARE "REASONABLE," AS THAT TERM IS DEFINED IN THE REGULATION. THE GENERAL SECRETARY'S COMPENSATION IS BASED ON THE TERMS IN THE GENERAL SECRETARY'S CONTRACT. THE GENERAL SECRETARY APPROVES SALARY INCREASES FOR THE EXECUTIVE MANAGEMENT TEAM WITHIN THE SALARY RANGES APPROVED BY THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS AND THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE OF TRF TRUSTEES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ROTARY FOUNDATION MAKES ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND AUDITED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC ON THE ORGANIZATION'S WEBSITE, WWW.ROTARY.ORG.
HIGHEST COMPENSATED EMPLOYEES THE HIGHEST COMPENSATED EMPLOYEES ARE DETERMINED BASED ON W-2 AMOUNTS FOR THE FILING AND RELATED ORGANIZATION, CONSISTENT WITH PRIOR YEAR.
GAIN ON CASH SURRENDER VALUE OF INSURANCE 19,555. CHANGE IN VALUE OF SPLIT-INTEREST AGREEMENTS -2,094,128. REVERSAL OF GRANT EXPENSES 6,460,402. ADJUSTMENT TO NET ASSETS -1,500.
$17,722 OF THE AMOUNT FROM COLUMN (C) MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES RELATES TO UBI TAX.
